top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 90, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Sea Otter Classic Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
61ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Sea Otter Classic Gran Fondo's paved road through coastal hills and agricultural forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 90 miles.
At Sea Otter Classic Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Sea Otter Classic Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
5,500ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 90 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
5,500ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Sea Otter Classic Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
The aerobic base that lets you ride paved road through coastal hills and agricultural at tempo for 90 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,500ft of climbing on paved road through coastal hills and agricultural does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: April in Monterey, California, USA can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Sea Otter Classic Gran Fondo. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Sea Otter Classic Gran Fondo’s 90 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Sea Otter Classic Gran Fondo requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved road through coastal hills and agricultural valley to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 90 miles of paved road through coastal hills and agricultural valley in Monterey, California, USA.
